client.h(3) Library Functions Manual client.h(3)

NAME

client.h - Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_manager_t
Global client manager structure for server-side client coordination.

Typedefs


typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Functions


client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char *client_ip, int port)
client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, const char *client_ip, bool start_threads)
Register a WebRTC client with the server. int start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization. int remove_client (server_context_t *server_ctx, const char *client_id)
client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *client_id)
Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t socket)
Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. void cleanup_client_media_buffers (client_info_t *client)
void cleanup_client_packet_queues (client_info_t *client)
void * client_receive_thread (void *arg)
void stop_client_threads (client_info_t *client)
int process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t *type, void **data, size_t *len, uint32_t *sender_id)
void process_decrypted_packet (client_info_t *client, packet_type_t type, void *data, size_t len)
void initialize_client_info (client_info_t *client)

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.

Detailed Description

Per-client state management and lifecycle orchestration.

This header provides server-specific client management functions. The client_info_t structure and network logging macros are defined in lib/network/client.h.

Definition in file src/server/client.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct server_context_t server_context_t

Function Documentation

client_info_t * add_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char * client_ip, int port)

Definition at line 616 of file src/server/client.c.

616                                                                                                           {
617   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
618   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
619   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
620   int slot = -1;
621   int existing_count = 0;
622   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
623     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
624       slot = i; // Take first available slot
625     }
626     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
627       existing_count++;
628     }
629   }
630 
631   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
632   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
633            slot);
634   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
635     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
636     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
637     if (send_result < 0) {
638       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
639     }
640     return NULL;
641   }
642 
643   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
644   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
645   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
646   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
647 
648   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
649   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
650   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
651   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
652     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
653     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
654     return NULL;
655   }
656   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
657 
658   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
659   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
660   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
661 
662   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
663   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
664   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
665   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
666   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
667   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
668     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
669     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
670     return NULL;
671   }
672 
673   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
674   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
675   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
676   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
677     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
678     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
679     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
680     return NULL;
681   }
682 
683   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
684   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
685   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
686   if (!audio_queue) {
687     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
688     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
689     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
690     return NULL;
691   }
692 
693   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
694   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
695   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
696   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
697     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
698     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
699     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
700     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
701     return NULL;
702   }
703 
704   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
705   if (!send_buffer) {
706     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
707     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
708     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
709     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
710     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
711     return NULL;
712   }
713 
714   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
715   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
716   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
717   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
718   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_RECHECK: About to check if slot is still available");
719 
720   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
721   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
722     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
723     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
724     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
725     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
726     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
727     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
728 
729     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
730     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
731     return NULL;
732   }
733 
734   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
735   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
736   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
737 
738   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
739   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
740   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
741 
742   client->socket = socket;
743   client->is_tcp_client = true;
744   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
745   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
746   client->port = port;
747   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
748   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
749   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
750   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
751   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
752   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
753 
754   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
755   client->crypto_initialized = false;
756 
757   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
758   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
759   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
760 
761   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
762   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
763   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
764   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
765   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
766   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
767   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
768 
769   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
770   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
771   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME");
772   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
773                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
774     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
775     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
776   }
777   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME: display_name=%s", client->display_name);
778 
779   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
780   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
781 
782   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
783   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
784 
785   // Register socket with tcp_server
786   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
787   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
788     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
789     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
790     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
791     goto error_cleanup;
792   }
793 
794   g_client_manager.client_count++;
795   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
796             new_client_id, slot);
797 
798   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
799   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
800 
801   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
802 
803   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
804   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
805 
806   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock BEFORE registering them
807   // register_client_info_atomics needs these mutexes to be initialized first
808   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
809     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
810     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
811     return NULL;
812   }
813 
814   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
815     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
816     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
817     return NULL;
818   }
819 
820   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking - DISABLED to prevent deadlock
821   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() call
822   // NAMED_REGISTER which tries to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock
823   // The debug_sync thread holds READ lock while printing, causing reader-writer deadlock
824   // These are debug-only features not critical for functionality
825   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
826   // register_client_info_atomics(client);
827   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
828 
829   // Audio buffer atomics registration disabled - same deadlock reason
830   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_START: client=%p", (void *)client);
831   // if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
832   //   audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
833   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_DONE");
834   // } else {
835   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_SKIPPED: incoming_audio_buffer is NULL");
836   // }
837 
838   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
839   // CRITICAL: Do this BEFORE crypto handshake to avoid deadlock with mixer thread
840   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_START: g_audio_mixer=%p, client->incoming_audio_buffer=%p", (void *)g_audio_mixer,
841            (void *)client->incoming_audio_buffer);
842   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
843     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_CALLING: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
844     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
845       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
846     } else {
847       log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SUCCESS: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
848 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
849       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
850 #endif
851     }
852   } else {
853     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SKIPPED: conditions not met");
854   }
855   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_DONE");
856 
857   // Create the client's persistent transport early (before handshake).
858   // It starts with NULL crypto; server_crypto_handshake() sets crypto_ctx after success.
859   client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, NULL);
860   if (!client->transport) {
861     log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
862     if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
863       log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
864     }
865     return NULL;
866   }
867   log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s (crypto pending handshake)", new_client_id);
868 
869   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
870   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
871   // The handshake uses client->transport and sets its crypto_ctx on success.
872   log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_START: About to call server_crypto_init()");
873   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
874     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_DONE: server_crypto_init() returned 0");
875     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
876     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
877     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
878     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
879     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
880       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
881                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
882       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
883     }
884 
885     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_START: About to call server_crypto_handshake()");
886     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
887     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DONE: result=%d", crypto_result);
888     if (crypto_result != 0) {
889       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
890       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
891         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
892       }
893       return NULL;
894     }
895 
896     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
897     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
898     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CLEAR_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_START");
899     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
900     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
901       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
902                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
903       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
904     }
905 
906     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
907 
908     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
909     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
910     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
911     //
912     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
913     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
914     packet_envelope_t envelope;
915     bool used_pending_packet = false;
916 
917     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
918       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
919       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
920                client->pending_packet_type);
921       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
922       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
923       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
924       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
925       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
926       used_pending_packet = true;
927 
928       // Clear pending packet fields
929       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
930       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
931       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
932     } else {
933       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
934       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
935 
936       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
937       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
938       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
939 
940       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
941       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
942       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
943                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
944 
945       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
946       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
947 
948       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
949         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
950         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
951           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
952         }
953         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
954           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
955         }
956         return NULL;
957       }
958     }
959 
960     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
961       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
962                 client->client_id);
963       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
964         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
965       }
966       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
967         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
968       }
969       return NULL;
970     }
971 
972     // Process the capabilities packet directly
973     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
974               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
975     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
976 
977     // Free the packet data
978     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
979       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
980     }
981     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
982   }
983 
984   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
985   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
986   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
987   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
988   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
989   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
990     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
991     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
992     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
993     return NULL;
994   }
995   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
996 
997   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
998   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
999     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
1000     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1001       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
1002     } else {
1003       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
1004     }
1005   }
1006 
1007   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1008   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1009   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_START: About to broadcast to all clients");
1010   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1011   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_DONE");
1012 
1013   // Register client atomics for debug sync inspection
1014   register_client_info_atomics(client);
1015 
1016   log_info("[TCP_DBG] ADD_CLIENT_RETURNING: client_id=%s, client=%p", client->client_id, (void *)client);
1017   return client;
1018 
1019 error_cleanup:
1020   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
1021   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1022   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1023   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1024   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1025   return NULL;
1026 }

    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN,
    MAX_CLIENTS, MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, mixer_add_source(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT, network_error_string(),
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, client_info::outgoing_video_buffer,
    packet_queue_create_with_pools(), packet_queue_destroy(),
    PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS, PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES,
    client_info::pending_packet_length,
    client_info::pending_packet_payload,
    client_info::pending_packet_type, client_info::port,
    receive_packet_secure(), remove_client(),
    rwlock_wrlock, rwlock_wrunlock, SAFE_FREE,

    SAFE_STRNCPY, client_info::send_buffer,
    client_info::send_buffer_size, client_info::send_mutex,

    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::session_client_id,
    server_context_t::session_host, session_host_add_client(),
    SET_ERRNO, set_socket_timeout(),
    client_info::shutting_down, client_info::socket,
    socket_send(), server_context_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), client_info::transport,
    packet_envelope_t::type, video_frame_buffer_create(), and
    video_frame_buffer_destroy().






client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t
  * server_ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip,
  bool start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1051 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1052                                                      {
1053   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1054     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1055     return NULL;
1056   }
1057 
1058   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1059 
1060   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1061   int slot = -1;
1062   int existing_count = 0;
1063   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1064     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1065       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1066     }
1067     // Count only active clients
1068     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1069       existing_count++;
1070     }
1071   }
1072 
1073   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1074   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1075     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1076     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1077               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1078     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1079     return NULL;
1080   }
1081 
1082   if (slot == -1) {
1083     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1084     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1085     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1086     return NULL;
1087   }
1088 
1089   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1090   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1091 
1092   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1093   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1094   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1095     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1096     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1097     return NULL;
1098   }
1099 
1100   // Initialize client
1101   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1102 
1103   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1104   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1105     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1106     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1107   }
1108   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1109     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1110     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1111   }
1112   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1113     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1114     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1115   }
1116   if (client->send_buffer) {
1117     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1118     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1119   }
1120 
1121   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1122 
1123   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1124   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1125   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1126   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1127   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1128   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1129   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1130   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1131   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1132   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1133   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1134   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1135   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1136   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1137   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1138 
1139   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1140   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1141   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1142   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1143   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1144   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1145   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1146 
1147   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1148   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1149   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1150   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1151 
1152   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1153   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1154 
1155   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1156   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1157   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1158     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1159     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1160     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1161   }
1162 
1163   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1164   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1165   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1166     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1167     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1168     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1169   }
1170 
1171   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1172   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1173   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1174     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1175     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1176   }
1177 
1178   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1179   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1180   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1181   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1182     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1183     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1184   }
1185 
1186   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1187   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1188   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1189     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1190     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1191   }
1192 
1193   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1194   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1195   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1196   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1197     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1198     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1199   }
1200 
1201   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1202   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1203             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1204 
1205   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1206   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1207   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1208 
1209   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1210   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1211   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1212 
1213   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1214   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1215     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1216       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1217     } else {
1218 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1219       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1220 #endif
1221     }
1222   }
1223 
1224   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1225   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1226     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1227     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1228     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1229     return NULL;
1230   }
1231 
1232   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1233   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1234     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1235     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1236     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1237     return NULL;
1238   }
1239 
1240   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1241   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1242     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1243     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1244     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1245     return NULL;
1246   }
1247 
1248   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1249   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1250   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1251   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1252 
1253   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1254   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1255   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1256   if (start_threads) {
1257     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1258     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1259       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1260       return NULL;
1261     }
1262     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1263     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1264   } else {
1265     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1266               new_client_id);
1267   }
1268 
1269   // Send initial server state to the new client
1270   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1271     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1272   } else {
1273 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1274     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1275 #endif
1276   }
1277 
1278   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1279   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1280   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1281   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1282 
1283   server_state_packet_t state;
1284   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1285   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1286   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1287 
1288   // Convert to network byte order
1289   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1290   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1291   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1292   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1293 
1294   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1295   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1296     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1297              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1298   } else {
1299     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1300               state.connected_client_count);
1301   }
1302 
1303   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1304   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1305   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1306     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1307     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1308       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1309     } else {
1310       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1311     }
1312   }
1313 
1314   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1315   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1316   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1317 
1318   return client;
1319 
1320 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1321   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1322   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1323   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1324   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1325   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1326   return NULL;
1327 }

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1790 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1790                                        {
1791   // Log thread startup
1792   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1793 
1794   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1795 
1796   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1797   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1798   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1799   if (!client) {
1800     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1801     return NULL;
1802   }
1803 
1804   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1805   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1806 
1807   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1808             client->is_tcp_client);
1809 
1810   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1811     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1812     return NULL;
1813   }
1814 
1815   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1816   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1817   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1818     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1819     return NULL;
1820   }
1821 
1822   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1823   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1824   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1825     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1826     return NULL;
1827   }
1828 
1829   // NOTE: Atomic registration disabled
1830   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() attempt
1831   // to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock, which conflicts with debug_sync
1832   // thread holding READ lock during printing, causing deadlock.
1833   // These are debug-only features, not critical for functionality.
1834   // The atomics work fine without being registered in the debug system.
1835 
1836   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1837   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1838   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1839 
1840   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1841 
1842   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1843   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1844   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1845 
1846   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1847            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1848 
1849   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1850     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1851     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1852     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1853       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1854       break;
1855     }
1856 
1857     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1858     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1859     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1860       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1861       break;
1862     }
1863 
1864     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1865     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1866     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1867 
1868     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1869       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1870       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1871           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1872 
1873       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1874       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1875         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1876         break;
1877       }
1878 
1879       // Handle receive errors
1880       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1881         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1882         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1883           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1884                     err_ctx.context_message);
1885           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1886             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1887             break;
1888           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1889             log_error_client(
1890                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1891             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1892             break;
1893           }
1894         }
1895         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1896                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1897         break;
1898       }
1899     } else {
1900       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1901       void *packet_data = NULL;
1902       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1903       size_t packet_len = 0;
1904 
1905       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1906       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1907       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1908       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1909       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1910         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1911         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1912                   client->client_id);
1913         break;
1914       }
1915       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1916       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1917 
1918       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1919                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1920 
1921       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1922           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1923 
1924       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1925       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1926         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1927         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1928           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1929           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1930           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1931               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1932             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1933             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1934             APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
1935             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1936             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1937           }
1938 
1939           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1940             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1941             break;
1942           }
1943         }
1944         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1945                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1946         break;
1947       }
1948 
1949       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1950                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1951 
1952       // Validate received packet before queueing
1953       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1954         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1955                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1956         if (allocated_buffer) {
1957           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1958         }
1959         continue;
1960       }
1961 
1962       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1963       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1964       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1965 
1966       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1967       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1968       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1969       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1970 
1971       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1972                payload_len, packet_len);
1973 
1974       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1975       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1976       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1977       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1978       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1979                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1980 
1981       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1982         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1983                   client->client_id);
1984         if (allocated_buffer) {
1985           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1986         }
1987       } else {
1988         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1989                  packet_len);
1990         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1991         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1992       }
1993     }
1994   }
1995 
1996   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
1997   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
1998   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
1999   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
2000   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
2001   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2002   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2003   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
2004   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
2005 
2006   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
2007   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
2008   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
2009   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2010   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
2011   if (server_ctx) {
2012     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
2013       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2014     }
2015   } else {
2016     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
2017   }
2018 
2019   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
2020 
2021   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2022   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2023 
2024   return NULL;
2025 }

    client_info::active, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_self(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    buffer_pool_free(), client_info::client_id,
    asciichat_error_context_t::code, cond_signal(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    client_info::dispatch_queue_cond, client_info::display_name,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_NETWORK, g_should_exit,
    HAS_ERRNO, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_tcp_client, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_error,
    log_error_client, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,

    client_info::protocol_disconnect_requested,
    client_info::receive_thread_id,
    client_info::received_packet_queue,
    acip_transport_methods_t::recv, remove_client(),
    client_info::send_mutex, client_info::send_thread_running,
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::shutting_down,
    client_info::socket, client_info::transport,
    packet_header_t::type, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.






client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 389 of file
  src/server/client.c.


389                                                         {
390   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
391     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
392     return NULL;
393   }
394 
395   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
396   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
397 
398   client_info_t *result = NULL;
399   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
400 
401   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
402 
403   if (!result) {
404     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
405   }
406 
407   return result;
408 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data

  src/server/client.c.

434   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
435 
436   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
437     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
438       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
439       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
440       return client;
441     }
442   }
443 
444   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
445   return NULL;
446 }

3761       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function

3781     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1329 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1329                                                                        {
1330   if (!server_ctx) {
1331     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1332     return -1;
1333   }
1334 
1335   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1336   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1337   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1338   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1339 
1340   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1341   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1342 
1343   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1344     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1345     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1346       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1347       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1348       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1349         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1350         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1351         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1352       }
1353       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1354       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1355       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1356       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1357       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1358       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1359       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1360       target_client = client;
1361 
1362       // Store display name before clearing
1363       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1364 
1365       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1366       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1367       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1368       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1369       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1370       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1371 
1372       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1373       if (client->audio_queue) {
1374         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1375       }
1376       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1377 
1378       break;
1379     }
1380   }
1381 
1382   // If client not found, unlock and return
1383   if (!target_client) {
1384     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1385     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1386     return -1;
1387   }
1388 
1389   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1390   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1391   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1392     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1393         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1394     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1395       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1396       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1397         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1398       } else {
1399         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1400                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1401       }
1402     } else {
1403       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1404     }
1405   }
1406 
1407   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1408   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1409   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1410 
1411   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1412   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1413   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1414   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1415   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1416   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1417             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1418 
1419   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1420     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1421     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1422     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1423     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1424       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1425       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1426         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1427         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1428       }
1429     } else {
1430       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1431     }
1432   } else if (target_client) {
1433     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1434     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1435 
1436     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1437     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1438     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1439       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1440     } else {
1441       void *recv_result = NULL;
1442       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1443       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1444         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1445       } else {
1446         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1447       }
1448     }
1449 
1450     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1451     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1452       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1453       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1454       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1455       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1456         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1457       } else {
1458         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1459       }
1460     }
1461 
1462     // Join send thread
1463     void *send_result = NULL;
1464     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1465     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1466       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1467     } else {
1468       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1469     }
1470     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1471     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1472   }
1473 
1474   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1475   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1476   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1477   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1478   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1479     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1480     target_client->transport = NULL;
1481     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1482   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1483     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1484     target_client->transport = NULL;
1485     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1486   }
1487 
1488   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1489   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1490     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1491     socket_close(client_socket);
1492   }
1493 
1494   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1495   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1496 
1497   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1498   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1499   if (target_client) {
1500     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1501     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1502     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1503       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1504                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1505       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1506       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1507     }
1508   }
1509 
1510   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1511   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1512     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1513     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1514     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1515     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1516   }
1517 
1518   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1519   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1520 
1521   // Remove from audio mixer
1522   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1523     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1524 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1525     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1526 #endif
1527   }
1528 
1529   // Remove from uthash table
1530   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1531   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1532   if (target_client) {
1533     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1534     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1535     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1536       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1537       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1538     } else {
1539       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1540                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1541     }
1542   } else {
1543     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1544   }
1545 
1546   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1547   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1548     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1549     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1550     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1551   }
1552 
1553   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1554   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1555   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1556   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1557   int retry_count = 0;
1558   const int max_retries = 5;
1559   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1560                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1561                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1562                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1563     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1564     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1565     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1566              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1567     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1568     retry_count++;
1569   }
1570 
1571   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1572     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1573               max_retries);
1574   }
1575 
1576   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1577   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1578   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1579   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1580   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1581   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1582 
1583   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1584   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1585   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1586 
1587   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1588   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1589   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1590   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1591   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1592   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1593 
1594   // Clear client structure
1595   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1596   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1597   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1598 
1599   // Recalculate client count
1600   int remaining_count = 0;
1601   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1602     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1603       remaining_count++;
1604     }
1605   }
1606   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1607 
1608   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1609             remaining_count);
1610 
1611   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1612 
1613   // Broadcast updated state
1614   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1615 
1616   return 0;
1617 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

2675   if (!server_ctx) {
2676     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2677     return -1;
2678   }
2679 
2680   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
2681   if (!client) {
2682     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2683     return -1;
2684   }
2685 
2686   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2687   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2690 of file

2690                                                 {
2691   if (!client) {
2692     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2693     return;
2694   }
2695 
2696   // Signal threads to stop
2697   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);

2707   }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager [extern]


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. This
    is the primary data structure for managing all connected clients. It serves
    as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and the per-client
    threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 336 of file
  src/server/client.c.






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock [extern]


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager. This lock
    enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 351 of file
  src/server/client.c.
